rulings.Case Title: NAVEEN SINGHA et al VS. NISSIM LANYADOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Deft'S Special Interrogatories And Req.'S For Documents, And Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf'S And Pltf'S Counsel For $4851.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT NISSIM LANYADOO'S Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories And Requests For Documents, And Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff's And Plaintiff's Counsel For $4851.00. Defendant's motion to compel responses to discovery is denied. Request for sanctions is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NAVEEN SINGHA et al VS. NISSIM LANYADOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFFS NAVEEN SINGHA, CASA FORTUNA LLC, CASA MANANA LLC'S Amended Motion For Protective Order Re Written Discovery. Plaintiff's motion for a protective order on written discovery is granted. Defendant must amend the request to be far less burdensome and more narrowly tailored. They must also not be repetitive. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFFS UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, JOEL SHAMITOFF, JOHN LUCERO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Continued to September 16, 2011. Moving party to provide a courtesy copy of its motion, and defendant to provide a courtesy copy of its opposition to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. ADI SIMAYOF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. Petition To Determine Validity Of Third Party Claim
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 10. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'S Petition To Determine Validity Of Third Party Claim. The petition to determine validity of third party claim is granted. The third party claim of Yedidia Simchaioff is valid. Plaintiff's argument is based solely on the declaration of counsel for plaintiff in the underlying matter that it was shown a document by defendant's counsel signed by claimant transferring his interest in the property to defendant. There is no copy of the document attached or other proof that the document exists. Regarding the claim of Yafit Shalom (defendant's wife), it has been shown that this is the homestead of Yasif and her children and there was no valid waiver of the exemption as applied to her and her children. Moreover, the court declines to determine the validity of Yafit Shalom's community property interest which is currently under the jurisdiction of the Family Court. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. ADI SIMAYOF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Application For Order For Sale Of Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 10. Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause Re: Application For Order For Sale Of Dwelling. Plaintiff's application for order for sale of dwelling is denied. Given the court's determination on the third party claims, there is not enough equity in the property to order the sale of the property. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: OLYMPIC VIEW REALTY, LLC VS. PARKMERCED INVESTORS PROPERTIES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Olympic View Realty, Llc Amended Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 11. Plaintiff Olympic View Realty, LLC'S Amended Motion For Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action is granted. Plaintiff has successfully met its burden. Defendant has failed to provide triable issues of fact regarding the definiteness of the contractual terms in question. The clear language of the contract does not support defendant's interpretation and the evidence shows defendant refused to execute new easements as required under the contract. Moreover, plaintiff need not wait too incur actual damages from a breach of a contract. E.O.C. Ord., Inc. v. Kavakovich (1988) 200 Cal.App. 2d 220, 231. Plaintiff is entitled to an award of attorney fees but must present that by separate motion setting forth amounts to be awarded. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE CHANG VS. JANE L. O'HARA GAMP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Of Plt'S Reconsideration For Leave To Amend The Fifth Cause Of Action "Bane Act Violation"

rulings.Case Title: DIMITRIOS STAVRAKIS VS. LILLIAN BAU LIN FAN AKA LILLIAN CHOU, AKA LILLIAN et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 6. DEFENDANT APR REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC'S Motion To Strike. Motion to strike prayer for attorney fees is denied. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts and theories to support this prayer. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MECHANICS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOHN T. KONTRABECKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment After Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 3. PLAINTIFF MECHANICS BANK'S Notice of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment After Hearing. Plaintiff's application for right to attach order is denied. The evidence does not show that winegrowing by defendant is his "trade." Moreover, if plaintiff forecloses on the property there will be sufficient funds to cover the outstanding indebtedness. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: FREEMAN LAW VS. GOOD VIEW LUMBER, A BUSINESS FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT GOOD VIEW ROOFING & BUILDING SUPPLY CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted with leave to amend, no opposition filed. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MAIN STREET ACQUISITION CORP. VS. ANTONIO RAMIREZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 25, 2011, line 8. DEFENDANT ANTONIO RAMIREZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff is exempt from filing with the Secretary of State as they are not transacting intrastate business. Plaintiff has provided sufficient facts to support its claims. Defendant must file answer within 15 days. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LILIAN CAPULI VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
